source_pr.title: planner: add skew risk for equals
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #60722

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

This enhancement is controlled by a new global/session variable tidb_opt_risk_eq_skew_ratio - that is also eligible for query level SET_VAR hint.

When an equal predicate value is NOT found in TopN, or as the last value in a histogram bucket - we will use the histogram rowcount (which excludes topN in stats version 2) divided by the remaining NDV. Thus, we assume that every remaining value is evenly distributed throughout the range. In reality, any single value could be skewed - since the topN values are limited to 100 values, and the histograms do not attempt to preference skewed values as the last in the bucket.

This enhancement will estimate the worst case skew for a value - which is (remaining count - remaining NDV) - but not to exceed the smallest value in the existing TopN. And then the tidb_opt_risk_eq_skew_ratio variable controls how much of that skew should be considered in the estimate - with 0 (the default) resulting in an estimate that matches the current count/ndv, and 1 == 100% of the skew.

This allows a user or external process to control the amount of skew should be considered in the optimizer's estimate when statistics are insufficient for estimating the exact value's selectivity.

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->
